title: SX1262.cs Renode RX is one-shot; needs continuous RX polling + RxDone IRQ
description: During Renode nRF52840 validation (project-LICHEN-r7h4.5), LoRa TX to lichen-sim was confirmed working (transmissions recorded with correct airtime), but firmware never receives frames. Root cause: SX1262.cs PollRx() is only invoked when the firmware issues the SetRx (0x82) opcode. The loramac-node/sx126x driver issues SetRx once to enter continuous RX, then waits for a DIO1 RxDone interrupt. SX1262.cs never polls lichen-sim again, so any packet arriving after the single SetRx poll is never delivered and no RxDone IRQ is raised. Fix: while the radio is in RX mode, poll the sim periodically (Renode timer / IHasOwnLife background loop) and raise the RxDone IRQ (DIO1) when a packet arrives, then stop on ClearIrqStatus/SetStandby. Must handle the sim time-model interaction (see sibling bead).
acceptance_criteria: 2-node Renode mesh in REALTIME sim mode shows receptions>0 and the receiving firmware logs/processes an inbound frame; RX validated with 3 codereview passes.
